content.full_text:
Dear Ms. Danforth:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Tiburon planning
Commissioners Richard Collins and Al Aguirre regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the.Act,,).r
QUESTTON
Farn PouucAI- PRACTTcES CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
December 28,2005
Ann R. Danforth
Town Attorney
Town of Tiburon
1505 Tiburon Boulevard
Tiburon, CA 94920
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-05-238
Are Commissioners Collins and Aguirre "legally required" to participate in
consideration of the Belvedere Tennis Club's request to amend its use permii where a
quorum cannot be convened due to the conflicts of interest of two commissioners and a
vacari seat on the planning commission?
CONCLUSION
Based on your facts, since a quorum cannot be convened due to two planning
commissioners with conflicts of interest and avacant seat on the commission, the town
may invoke legally required participation and either Commissioner Collins or Aguirre
may participate.
FACTS
The Belvedere Tennis Club ("BTC") has applied to the Town of Tiburon for an
amendment to its existing use permit. The BTC is a private recreational club. The
amendment would allow the BTC to expand its existing facility, changing the design of
I Government Code sections 81000 - 9l}l4. Commission regulations appearat Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
lrlFcB


File No. A-05-238
Page No. 2
the previously approved clubhouse and exercise building and making a number of
changes to the overall project layout (e.g., enlarging and moving the swimmingpool,
moving a spa, a different location for an exercise room and pool equipment building;.
The town's planning commission is the decision-making body for the BTC,s
application. Normally, the Commission has five members, but one resigned recently due
to illness. Of the remaining four commissioners, two are members of G BTC. One of
these member-commissioners also lives within 500 feet of the club. To fund construction
of the improvements, members will have to pay alincreased assessment of $3500 and
their monthly dues will increase by $45.00 per month. In addition, if the BTC is ever
dissolved, the club assets will be distributed among the members.
Under normal circumstances, you would advise the two member-commissioners
that they may not participate in the consideration of this project. However, because of
the vacancy, the commission will not have a quorum for considering the application
unless one of the members participates. Under the California Permii Streamlining Act,
you are required to make a decision on the project on or before January 20,2006.
(Section 65950.) The town cannot fill the vacancy on the planning commission by that
date. In compliance with state law, the town must advertise.rnr"lrrd.rl"d vacancies for
several weeks, and hold interviews with potential candidates before making an
appointment. Historically, it has taken over a month to make an appointmJnt; more
recently it has taken two or three months. The commission should 6egin hearings on the
BTC application in December to be able to make a decision by the permit Streamlining
Act deadline - the project is controversial and will likely require multiple meetings. y:ou
would like to select one of the member-commissioners by lot to participate in the
decision, in order to have a quorum.
In your telephone message of December2l,z}Ol,your staff confirmed that you
were authoizedto request advice on behalf of the two planning commissioners,
specifically Commissioners Richard Collins and Al Aguirre.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from makin g, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a govemmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest. The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard
analysis for deciding whether an official has a disqualifying conflict oflnteresi.2
(Regulation 18700(b)(1) - (S).) Your question concern" orrly the eighth step of the
commission standard analysis and ouiresponse is limited accordingly. 
^
2 This eight-step analysis is found atregulation 18700, subdivisions (bX1) - (g)), and is discussed
in the enclosed Commission publication, "Can I vote? overview of the Conflict La*..i,'


File No. ,{-05-238
Page No. 3
Step 8. The ,,LegaIIy Required particip ation, Exception
Section 87101 permits an official who is otherwise disqualified from making a
govemmental decision to participate in the decision when the official,. furti"ipution is
legally required. The rule does not apply when there is an alternative source of
decisionmaking consistent with the statute afihoizingthe decision. (Regulation 1g70g.)
Thus,.it only applies_when it is legally impossible forLe decision to be made without the
participation of the disqualified officiar. -orrseq,.r"rtl5 it does not apply when the
disqualified official's vote is merelyneeded to bieak i'ti" orwhen r;;*; 
can be
convened of other members of the town council who are not disqualified, whether or not
such other members are actuallypresent at the time of the disquaiific ation.
The "legallyrequired participation" rule is construed na:rowly. (Regulation
18708(c)') Consequently, the rule only allows the participation of the smallest number of
disqualified persons necessary to consiitute a quorum . (Ii re Hudson Ogigl4 Fppc
ops' 13') The best random method of selecting which disqualified member should
pafiicipate is bv lot. other means of random sJlection flruiu,'i-p;i;i; 
"quitable
may also be used. Whatevermethod is used, all disqualified official. -r.ip".ticipate in
the random selection and all must have an equal likeiihood of being choserr.' (Heisinger
Advice Letter, No. 4-95-333.)
once it is determined which disqualified official will participate in a decision, that
official is selected for the duration of thi proceedings in all related matters unless the
legal necessity for legally required participatior, ."u"r". to exist. A disqualified official
who participates under the authority of section 87101 may participate fully in the matter,
including taking part in deliberations and voting in open ,"rrioo*-orrrr" iJay d in such
closed sessions as are required by law.
The problem the planning commission is facingis that the conflicts of interests of
Commissioners collins and Aguirre do not deprive the-commission of a quorum. when
the vacant seat is counted with the two commissioners without conflicts of interest, the
commission theoretically has three members who could participate. Howeve., you argue,
that since the t'acancy cannot be filled soon enough, the commil;;;;; 
to convone
a quorum on this issue.
In determining whether the exception applies in context of a vacanc y, earlyadvice
letters drew a distinction between circumstances where it would be legally impossible to
{ill the vacant seats, and where the seats were merel y vacantuntil filled by the appointing
power. (Hunter Advice Letter, No. A-99-0gg.) However, in a 1999 opi"io",irr"
Commission signaled a change from this prior advice and did not allow the distinction
alone to dictate the applicability of the exception. Instead, the commission examined a
number of factors and concluded, in the case before it, thitthese factorc pro-ft"a
application of the exception, even when the vacancy was the result of the political failure
gf the appointing authority to fill the vacancy. (In ie Tobias (lggg) 13 Fppc op. 5.) The
Commission stated:


File No. A-05-238
Page No. 4
uu"*,"i,\3"[:ffiil?"xT:1h,Tillrfl3iTf #,,},:311.#,l,li,o.*
the nature of the decision; whether there was an alternative methoJof
decisionmaking consistent with 
_the purpose and functio"r "i,rr" prrticular
agency, *h:t!o the agency cou.rd have changed the q.ro**r"qri[*"rrtr,
or appointed alternative or interim members *ho 
"o.rid rote; *heth", th"
decision had to be made within a specified time period; and ihe
importance of the agerrcy moving forward.,,
1. The nature of the decision.
Amendment of a use permit is within the particurar expertise of the
planning commission.
2. whether there w&s on arternative method of decisionmaking
consistent with the purpose andfunctions of the parti"itn, agency. 
o
You have not identified any other altemate source of decision on such a
matter.
3. whether the agency courd hau_e changed the quorum requirements,
or appointed alternative or tnterim members wio could'vote.
You stated the town cannot fill_the vacancyon the planning commission
by the required date. You have not indicated that'thetown has a mechanism to
change the quorum requirements for this vote, or uppomt at"*ative or interim
members who can vote.
4- lYhether the decision had to be made within a specified time period;
Under the california permit streamlinin g Act,you are required to make a
decision on theproject on orbeforeJanuary 20,2006.
5. The importance of the agency movtngforward.
Failure to act may be deemed approval. (Section 65956.)
Based on the totality of the facts and circumstances, either Commissioner collins
or Aguirre may be le€ally required to participate in the decision uno .uyU" seiected to
do so, as described above. However, ihe selicted commissioner must disclose on the
record the existence of the financial inte-rest, describe with particularity the nature of the
economic interest giving rise to the conflict of interest, una airctor" th" r.gritu.i, ro,
concluding that there is no alternative source of decision. (Regulation l g70g(b).)


File No. 4-05-238
Page No. 5
,rr_ru{olou 
have any other questions regardingthis matter, please contactme at (gt6)
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
Enclosure
